About V3 LifeCare Exim

Connecting international buyers with qualified Indian manufacturing partners, backed by regulatory expertise and professional export coordination.

V3 Life Care Exim is a pharmaceutical merchant exporter specialising in sourcing, regulatory documentation, and export coordination for global markets. Every product inquiry is supported by a carefully selected WHO-GMP certified manufacturing network and an experienced in-house regulatory team, helping buyers make informed sourcing decisions with complete documentation support.

What You Can Expect:

  • •Products sourced through qualified WHO-GMP certified manufacturing partners
  • •In-house regulatory documentation support
  • •Professional export coordination
  • •Clear communication from inquiry to shipment
Read More

Why Partner with us?

We help pharmaceutical importers source qualified products, meet regulatory requirements, and ship with certainty, without the complexity of managing multiple suppliers.

Reliable Third-Party Manufacturing Network

Every product V3 sources comes from a verified network of WHO-GMP certified manufacturers. These are not directory listings; every facility entered our network through direct professional relationships built over 15 years in pharmaceutical regulatory affairs. When you place an order, the manufacturer behind it has already been evaluated for compliance, documentation capability, and supply reliability.

Strict Vendor Selection Process

Not every WHO-GMP certified facility meets V3's sourcing standards. Before any manufacturer enters our network, we evaluate their certification status, production capability, documentation track record, and quality systems. This process protects every buyer; you receive a product backed by a facility we have already vetted, not one we are trying for the first time.

Regulatory Documentation Support

Our in-house regulatory affairs team prepares every document your importing authority requires: CTD/ACTD dossiers, COPP, Free Sale Certificates, country-specific labelling and artwork. This work is done before your order ships, not after a customs query forces the issue. Documentation failures at the border are what V3 was specifically built to prevent.

Competitive Pricing on Every Order

Because V3 sources from multiple verified manufacturers, you receive pricing options instead of a single take-it-or-leave-it quote. Every quotation includes unit cost, manufacturer source, lead time, and documentation status. Full information before you commit. No hidden charges, no revisions after the order is placed.

Logistics & Shipment Coordination

Every shipment is coordinated from manufacturer to destination with GDP-compliant packaging, freight management, and customs clearance support handled by our team. Documentation is complete before dispatch, not assembled in response to a delay. Your order moves when it is supposed to, with everything it needs to clear.

Customer-Focused Export Coordination

Every order at V3 is managed by a dedicated team from the moment your inquiry arrives to the moment your shipment is delivered. Sourcing, documentation, freight, and customs coordination are handled under one roof with no handoffs between departments. You have a single point of contact who knows your order, your market, and your documentation requirements throughout the entire process.

How We Source and Export Pharmaceuticals

Understanding Your Requirements

We begin by understanding your specific product requirements, including formulation, strength, dosage form, packaging specifications, quantity, and destination country regulations. The more precisely we understand your requirement upfront, the more accurate and complete our quotation will be.

Sourcing from Qualified Manufacturing Partners

Based on your requirements, we source products from carefully selected pharmaceutical manufacturers that hold valid manufacturing licences and comply with applicable quality and regulatory standards. All partners are selected based on their product-specific capabilities and export experience.

Manufacturer & Documentation Verification

Before procurement is confirmed, our team verifies the selected manufacturer's credentials: manufacturing licences, GMP certifications, product registrations, quality assurance systems, and production capabilities. We also review all required quality documents including Certificate of Analysis, GMP Certificate, Product Specifications, and Stability Data where required by your importing country. You receive written confirmation before production begins.

Procurement & Quality Review

Once your order is confirmed, we procure the required products from approved manufacturing partners under mutually agreed commercial terms. Before dispatch, products undergo quality verification through manufacturer-provided quality documentation and, where required, independent inspection to ensure consistency and compliance with your specifications and applicable regulatory standards.

Export Documentation Preparation

We coordinate and prepare all necessary export documentation required for your destination country: Commercial Invoice, Packing List, Certificate of Origin, Bill of Lading or Airway Bill, Certificate of Analysis, COPP, Free Sale Certificate, and any country-specific compliance documents. Every document is reviewed against your importing country's requirements before shipment is released.

Logistics, Delivery & Post-Shipment Support

We work with reliable logistics partners to arrange transportation, customs clearance, and shipment of pharmaceutical products to international destinations, maintaining product integrity throughout the supply chain. Our team provides shipment tracking, documentation support, and post-shipment assistance for a smooth delivery process and ongoing procurement requirements.

Certifications & Compliance

Our manufacturing partners hold recognised certifications and regulatory approvals to support pharmaceutical exports across global markets.

WHO GMP (May 2027)
Cambodia (Oct 2027)
Yemen (Dec 2027)
Philippines (Sept 2027)

Global Presence

Our Products Range

All products sourced from qualified manufacturing partners with export documentation available on inquiry.

Tablets

Tablets are the most widely used solid dosage form in pharmaceutical exports. Available in immediate-release, sustained-release, and coated variants, they deliver precise doses across a broad range of therapeutic categories.

Syrups

Pharmaceutical syrups are oral liquid formulations designed for accurate dosing and ease of administration. Available for paediatric and adult use, they are preferred where solid dosage forms are not suitable.

Ointment

Ointments are semi-solid topical formulations applied to the skin for dermatological and therapeutic purposes. Available across a range of active ingredients and therapeutic categories.

Respules

Respules are unit-dose liquid formulations sealed in plastic ampoules, designed for use with nebulisers in respiratory care. Commonly prescribed for conditions including asthma and COPD.

Capsules

Capsules are available in hard-shell and soft-gel formulations across a broad range of therapeutic categories. They provide accurate dosing and are widely accepted in international pharmaceutical markets.

Injection

Injectable formulations are sterile preparations used across hospital and clinical settings. Available as vials, ampoules, and lyophilised products for oncology, critical care, anaesthesia, and emergency medicine.

Download Product Catalogue

Our Services

01
Pharmaceutical Export Operations

From your initial inquiry to final delivery, we coordinate every stage of the pharmaceutical export process. Our team manages communication, order coordination, documentation flow, and shipment execution from a single point of contact.

  • ↳Inquiry-based product sourcing across therapeutic categories
  • ↳WHO-GMP certified manufacturer network
  • ↳GDP-compliant packaging and handling for all shipments
  • ↳Sample export coordination with no regulatory hurdles at origin
  • ↳Smooth domestic transportation from manufacturer to port of export
  • ↳Pre-shipment quality inspection at dispatch point
02
Regulatory & Dossier Preparation

Our in-house regulatory affairs team prepares every dossier and submission document your importing authority requires. Documentation is prepared and reviewed before the export process begins, supporting a smoother regulatory review.

  • ↳CTD/ACTD dossier preparation for multiple markets
  • ↳Product registration support
  • ↳Country-specific labelling and artwork compliance
  • ↳Free Sale Certificate, COPP, and COA preparation
  • ↳In-house review of all documentation before submission
  • ↳Ongoing regulatory updates for target markets
03
Export Documentation Support

Every shipment is supported by a complete export documentation package aligned with destination market requirements.

  • ↳Certificate of Analysis (COA)
  • ↳Certificate of Pharmaceutical Product (COPP)
  • ↳Free Sale Certificate
  • ↳Packing list, commercial invoice, and shipping documentation
  • ↳Market-specific import compliance documents
  • ↳Complete documentation package verified before dispatch
04
Global Product Sourcing

Tell us what you need, and we'll identify the most suitable manufacturing partner based on your product, regulatory, and market requirements. Every sourcing recommendation is backed by verified manufacturer credentials and documentation support.

  • ↳Finished formulations across multiple therapeutic categories
  • ↳API sourcing on inquiry
  • ↳Verified manufacturer credentials available on request
  • ↳Transparent, competitive pricing with fast quotation turnaround
  • ↳Flexible MOQ depending on product and manufacturer
05
Supply Chain & Logistics Coordination

We coordinate the full logistics chain from the manufacturing facility to your destination market, helping keep shipments on schedule while meeting international shipping requirements.

  • ↳Freight coordination and carrier selection
  • ↳Temperature-sensitive and cold chain packaging where required
  • ↳Customs clearance support at origin
  • ↳End-to-end shipment tracking
  • ↳Domestic logistics managed from manufacturer facility to export port
06
Market Entry Guidance

Before you commit to a product or market, we help you understand what regulatory pathway applies, what documentation your authority requires, and whether your product is viable for that market. This advisory happens before sourcing begins.

  • ↳Regulatory pathway assessment for your target market
  • ↳Documentation requirements identified before order placement
  • ↳Product viability check against destination market standards
  • ↳Guidance on dossier format and health authority expectations
  • ↳Pre-order compliance review to avoid delays later

Testimonials

★★★★★

"V3 provided a complete documentation package aligned with DIGEMID requirements on the first submission. Our import process in Peru was the smoothest we have had with any Indian exporter."

Procurement Director
Pharmaceutical Importer · Lima, Peru
★★★★★

"We placed a trial order to verify quality before committing to volume. V3 handled the MOQ with no pressure and provided WHO-GMP certificates and COA without us needing to follow up. We are now in discussions for a repeat order."

Supply Chain Manager
Pharmaceutical Distributor · Lagos, Nigeria
★★★★★

"Regulatory documentation from Indian exporters is usually our biggest headache. V3 is the first exporter where I have spoken directly to a regulatory affairs professional during the inquiry. That gave us confidence to proceed."

Medical Procurement Officer
Government Health Authority · Nairobi, Kenya
